publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-10 18:19:06 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: International War Crimes Tribunal records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: International War Crimes Tribunal (1st ) (Location of meeting: Stockholm, Sweden and Roskilde, Denmark). Date of meeting or treaty signing: 1967 :.)

did.abstract: Tribunal records include mimeographs, edited versions, and final drafts of testimonies, reports, opening and closing statements, and summaries of the Stockholm and Copenhagen sessions (1967); also, press releases, copies of correspondence, photographs, speeches, agendas, and statutes. In addition, there are materials pertaining to the National Liberation Front; reports of the Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam (1970); and reports, printed matter, and other materials concerning the International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: The International War Crimes Tribunal was sponsored by the Bertrand Russell Peace Foundation to provide a forum for testimony and evidence concerning the damaging effects of U.S. policy in Indochina.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Tribunal records include mimeographs, edited versions, and final drafts of testimonies, reports, opening and closing statements, and summaries of the Stockholm and Copenhagen sessions (1967); also, press releases, copies of correspondence, photographs, speeches, agendas, and statutes. In addition, there are materials pertaining to the National Liberation Front; reports of the Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam (1970); and reports, printed matter, and other materials concerning the International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: International War Crimes Tribunal records, #4285. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: War -- Protection of civilians.
War (International law)
War crimes.
Vietnamese Conflict, 1961-1975 -- Chemical warfare.
Vietnamese Conflict, 1961-1975 -- Atrocities.
Prisoners of war.
Biological warfare.
controlaccess.title: Against the crime of silence: proceedings of the Russell International War Crimes Tribunal.
controlaccess.corpname: Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam
International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indochina
Mạ̮t trận dân tộc giải phóng miè̂n nam Việt Nam
Vietnam (Democratic Republic). Ủy ban điè̂u tra tội ác chi é̂n tranh của đè̂ quó̂c Mỹ ở Việt-Nam

did.unittitle: I. International War Crimes Tribunal - The Evidence of Stockholm,
Editor's Preface. Duffett, John.
Inaugural Statement. Sartre, Jean Paul.
Inaugural Statement to the First Tribunal Session. Russell, Bertrand.
Correspondence with heads of state. Gaulle, Charles de; Sartre, Jean Paul.
"Trial of Japanese War Criminals - Tokoyo 1946-1948 ," testimony by Jean Chesneaux. Chesneaux, Jean.
"Summary of Historical Report." Kolko, Gabriel.
"American Intervention in Vietnam - 1954-1964 ," testimony by Jean Chesneaux. Chesneaux, Jean.
"Historical Report on U.S. Aggression in Vietnam - 1964-1967 ," testimony by Charles Fourniau. Fourniau, Charles.
"International Law and the Military Draft," testimony by Stanley Faulkner. Faulkner, Stanley.
"Summary of a Juridical Report," by Samuel Rosenwein. Rosenwein, Samuel.
"Judicial Report of Aggression in Vietnam," testimony by the Japanese Legal Committee. Japan Committee for the Investigation of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam.
"Report from Cambodia," testimony by Bernard Couret. Couret, Bernard.
"Report from Cambodia and North Vietnam," testimony by Tarig Ali. Ali, Tarig.
"On Cambodia," testimony by Mme. Rena Kahn. Kahn, Rena.
"Testimony on Cambodia," by Wilfred Burchett, Burchett, Wilfred G.
"Questioning of a Khmer Mercenary." Kahn, M. Francis.
"Extracts from a Summary Report on the Bombing of the Civil Population in the North," testimony by Abraham Behar, M.D. Behar, Abraham.
"Non-Military Targets and Methods of Attack," testimony by Y. Ishijima. Ishijima, Y.
"Bombardment of Civilians in North Vietnam," testimony by John Takman, M.D. and Axel Hojer, M.D. Hojer, Axel; Takman, John.
"American Bombing in North Vietnam," testimony by Jean Michel Krivine, M.D. Krivine, Jean Michel.
"On the Destruction of the Leprosarium of Quinh Lap," testimony by M. Francis Kahn. Kahn, M. Francis.
"Report on Civilian Bombardment," testimony by Professor S. Kugai. Kugai, S.
"Report from North and South Vietnam," testimony by Marta Rojas. Rojas, Martha.
"Extracts from the Testimony of Alejo Carpentier." Carpentier, Alejo.
"Investigations of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam," testimony by Lawrence Daly. Daly, Lawrence.
"Report on Bombing of North Vietnam," testimony by Charles Cobb and Julius Lester. Birnstingl, Martin; Cobb, Charles; Lester, Julius.
"Effects of the Aggression Against the DRV on Public Health," testimony by Roberto Guerra, M.D. Guerra, Roberto.
"Testimony and Medical Report of Vietnamese Victims."
"Examinations of Wounded from U.S.Bombing," testimony by Henrik Fross, M.D. Fross, Henrik.
"Report on the Destruction of Dikes: Holland 1944-1945 and Korea 1953 ," testimony by Professor Gabriel Kolko. Kolko, Gabriel.
"Significance of the Destruction of Dikes in North Vietnam," testimony by Professor Fujio Yamazaki. Yamazaki, Fujio.
"Some Facts on Bombing of Dikes," testimony by Professor Makato Kandachi. Kandachi, Makato.
"Bombing of Dikes and Irrigation Systems in the DRV," testimony by Tsetsure Tsurushima. Tsurushima, Tsetsure.
"On the Law of Land Warfare," testimony by Frank Pestana. Pestana, Frank.
"Extracts from 'Fundamentals of Aerospace Weapons Sysytems'"
"Technical Aspects of Fragmentation Bombs," testimony by Jean Pierre Vigier, M.D. Vigier, Jean Pierre.
"Combined Report on Anti-Personnel Bombs," testimony by Members of the Japanese Scientific Committee. Japan Committee for the Investigation of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam.
"Effects of Anti-Personnel Bombs on the Human Body," testimony by F. Mazas and J. Zucman. Mazas, F.; Zucman, J.
"Statement on the Pentagon's Denial of the Use of CBUs," by Vladimir Dedijer. Dedijer, Vladimir.
"Report from North Vietnam on Civil Bombardment," testimony by J.B. Nielands.
"The Bombing of Dai Lai," testimony by Gerard Chaliand. Chaliand, Gerard.
"Verdict of the Stockholm Session."
"Closing Address to the Stockholm Session," by Bertrand Russell. Russell, Bertrand.
II. International War Crimes Tribunal - The Evidence of Copenhagen,
"Opening Statement to the Second Tribunal Session," by Bertrand Russell. Russell, Bertrand.
"Opening Address to the Second Session," by Jean Paul Sartre. Sartre, Jean Paul.
"Greetings to the Tribunal from American Supporters," address by Carl Oglesby. Oglesby, Carl.
"Report on the Law of War," testimony by Yves Jouffa. Jouffa, Yves.
"Incendiary Weapons, Poision Gas, Defoliants Used in Vietnam," testimony by Abraham Behar, M.D. Behar, Abraham.
"On Chemical and Biological Warfare in Vietnam," testimony by Alexander Minkowski, M.D. Minkowski, M.D.
"Report of the Sub-Committee on Chemical Warfare in Vietnam." Lederer, Edgar.
"Extracts from a Report on Agricultural Chemicals Used in Vietnam," testimony by the Japanese Scientific Committee. Japan Committee for the Investigatio of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam.
"Patterns of Bombing Civilans in the North," testimony by Wilfred Burchett. Burchett, Wilfred G.
"Escalating Bombardment of North Vietnamese Cities," testimony by Antonello Trombadori. Trambadori, Antonello.
"Judicial Report on the Treatment of War Prisoners and Civilians," testimony by Solange Bouvier Ajam. Ajam, Sloange Bouvier.
"Testimony and Questioning of David Kenneth Tuck." Tuck, David Kenneth.
"Testimony and Questioning of Peter Martinsen." Martinsen, Peter.
"Testimony and Questioning of Donald Duncan." Duncan, Donald.
"Report of the Commission of Inquiey to the United States," testimony by Gisele Halimi. Halimi, Gisele.
"Study on the Erosion of Moral Constraint in the Vietnam War," testimony complied by Clergy and Laymen concerned about Vietnam. Clergy and Laymen concerned about Vietnam.
"A Doctor Reports from South Vietnam," testimony by Erich Wullf. Wullf, Erich.
"Report on American Conduct of the War in the South," testimony by Jean Bertolino. Bertolino, Jean.
"On Treatment of Civilians," testimony by Joris Ivens and Marceline Loridon. Ivens, Joris; Loridon, Marceline.
"On the Treatment of Women and Children in the South," testimony by Madeleine Riffaud. Riffaud, Madeleine.
"Imprsionment and Torture of Political Prisioners," testimony by Pham Thi Yen. Pham Thi Yen.
"Depositions Taken Concerning South Vietnam," from testimony by Hugh Manes. Manes, Hugh.
"Report of the Seventh Inquiry Commission Concerning the Zones in the South under NLF Control," testimony by Roger Pic. Pic, Roger.
"Summary Report on Complicity of Thailand and the Philippines," from testimony by Charles Fourniau. Fourniau, Charles.
"Complicity of Thailand in the U.S. Aggression," testimony of the Japanese Commission. Japan Committee for the Investigation of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam.
"Combined Report on the Complicity of Japan in the Vietnam War," testimony by the Japanese Committee. Japan Committee for the Investigation of U.S. War Crimes in Vietnam.
"The United States and Laos," testimony by Wilfred Burchett. Burchett, Wilfred G.
"U.S. Actions against Laos," testimony by Lt. Guillermo Frank Llanes. Llanes, Guillermo Frank.
"On Genecide," by Jean Paul Sartre. Sartre, Jean Paul.
"Summation on Genocide," by Lelio Basso. Basso, Lelio.
"Summary and Verdict of the Second Session."
"Appeal to American and World Opinion," read by Dave Dellinger.
"Bertrand Russell's Final Address to the Tribunal, Copenhagen, Dec. 1967 ." Russell, Bertrand.
III. Miscellaneous Materials relating to the International War Crimes Tribunal
Miscellaneous Vietnamese sources.
National Liberation Front statements and testimony. National Liberation Front.
National Liberation Front reports. National Liberation Front.
Reports of the Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam.. Commission for the Denunciation of U.S. War Crimes in South Vietnam.
Report by Dr. Pam Ngoc Thach. Pham Ngoc Thanch.
Press releases.
Miscellaneous statements and speeches regarding the Russell War Crimes Tribunal.
Agendas, statutes, and other materials pertaining to the Tribunal.
Reports on genocide. Aybar, Mehmet Ali; Matarasso, L.; Alvarez Tabio, Fernando.
Testimonies and reports not appearing in Against the Crime of Silence: Proceedings of the Russell International War Crimes Tribunal. Caldwell, Malcom; Lacostw, Yves.
Testimonies and reports not appearing in Against the Crime of Silence: Proceedings of the Russell International War Crimes Tribunal. Ilkon, Paik; Matarasso, L.
Testimonies and reports by individuals for whom other reports appear in Against the Crime of Silence. Burchett, Wilfred G.; Pic, Roger.
Testimonies and reports by individulas for whom other reports appear in Against the Crime of Silence. Cini, Marcello; Halimi, Gisele; Kahn, M. Francis; Llanes, Guillermo Frank.
Japanese evidence (see alos various testimonies in Against the Crime of Silence). Funazaki, Zenzaburo; Yoshizawa, Hiroki; Nagoya People's Tribunal; Tokyo Tribunal.
Japanese evidence (see also various testimonies in Against the Crime of Silence). Shibata Shingo.
Cambodian evidence. Thiounn Prasith; Norodom Sihanouk, Prince.
Laos. Kaysone Phomvihane; Thanh Savanvilay.
IV. International Commission of Enquiry into U.S. Crimes in Indo china - Stockholm,
Materials from "Republic of South Vietnam." Provisional Revolutionary Governemnt of South Vietnam.
Democratic Republic of Vietnam materials. Provisional Revolutionary Government of South Vietnam.
Reports from Western sources. Frank, Hans Goran; Poltorak, Arkady; Provisional Revolutionary Governemnt of South Vietnam.
Published materials. Provisional Revolutionary Governemnt of South Vietnam.
V. Photographs
Prints and negatives taken during the International War Crimes Tribunal.
